data.title: Wells Fargo: U.S. core inflation in March will be the lowest this year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Wells Fargo economist Sarah House is looking ahead to the US March CPI: March's CPI data may appear a little lagging. But it should give us an idea of ​​how the changing trade environment is beginning to affect prices. March looks set to be the low point for core inflation this year as tariffs lead to faster price increases. Coupled with growing concerns about a self-brewed recession in the United States, the Federal Reserve remains in "wait-and-see" mode on interest rates. Although there are still many uncertainties about the duration, transmission effects and second-round price impacts of these tariffs, the trend is clear. The current trade policy "brings new challenges to controlling inflation." For more than a year, the Fed's final stage of the fight against inflation has been difficult, highlighting the difficulty of completely eliminating inflation without significantly weakening demand. (Jinshi)

data.title: March CPI Data May Indicate Core Inflation Low Point Amid Trade Challenges
data.description: According to Odaily, Wells Fargo economist Sarah House has commented on the upcoming March CPI data for the United States, suggesting it may appear somewhat delayed. However, it is expected to provide insights into how the evolving trade environment is beginning to impact prices. With tariffs accelerating price increases, March is likely to represent the low point for core inflation this year. Coupled with growing concerns over a self-induced economic recession in the U.S., this situation keeps the Federal Reserve in a 'wait-and-see' stance regarding interest rates. Although uncertainties remain about the duration of these tariffs, their transmission effects, and secondary price impacts, the trend is clear. The current trade policy presents new challenges for controlling inflation. For over a year, the final phase of the Federal Reserve's battle against inflation has been fraught with difficulties, highlighting the challenge of eliminating inflation without significantly weakening demand.

data.title: China Intensifies Efforts to Combat Illegal Financial Activities in 2025
data.description:
According to PANews, a comprehensive meeting focused on preventing and combating illegal financial activities was held in Beijing on April 10, 2025. The event, which included the Inter-Ministerial Joint Conference and a special action meeting on tackling illegal fundraising, emphasized the need to systematically reduce existing risks and address challenges in handling such activities. 

The conference outlined several key strategies, including tightening market entry regulations, enhancing monitoring and warning systems, improving administrative handling mechanisms, and strengthening public education and guidance. These measures aim to consolidate and advance the achievements made in combating illegal financial activities, ensuring a successful conclusion to the special action initiatives. 

Participants were urged to deepen comprehensive policy measures to address the root causes of illegal financial activities, reflecting China's ongoing commitment to maintaining financial stability and security.

data.title: VanEck Submits Application for Avalanche ETF Listing on Nasdaq
data.description: According to BlockBeats, VanEck has submitted an application to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for the listing of an Avalanche ETF on Nasdaq. This move marks a significant step in expanding investment opportunities in the cryptocurrency sector. The application is part of VanEck's ongoing efforts to offer diverse financial products that cater to the growing interest in digital assets.

data.title: BitMart will launch mind network (FHE)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch mind network (FHE) at 20:00 (Eastern Time Zone 8) on April 10, and the FHE/USDT trading pair will be opened.

data.title: Bitget's unified account function opens real-time trading to invited users
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, Bitget Exchange announced that its unified account function has begun to open real-time trading rights to invited users. This function is mainly aimed at institutional users and professional traders, aiming to comprehensively improve the flexibility and execution efficiency of transactions. The unified account supports the automatic portfolio margin mechanism, and the profits and losses between different trading products can be consolidated and settled to achieve unified management and optimize the overall fund scheduling and risk control capabilities.
The real-time trading of the unified account has been opened since April 7, and currently supports 15 trading pairs including PEPEUSDT, ADAUSDT, DOGEUSDT, LINKUSDT, and WIFUSDT. The new function currently adopts an invitation system, giving priority to professional traders with Maker strategies.
Bitget CEO Gracy Chen said; "Institutional business is one of our key layouts this year. The unified account has a structural advantage in improving trading flexibility and capital utilization efficiency, and will play a key role in serving institutional users. In addition, the system execution efficiency is also improved simultaneously, and the overall execution speed is 20% faster than traditional accounts."

data.title: Hassett: Bond market turmoil adds 'a little more urgency' to suspend tariffs
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Hassett, director of the U.S. National Economic Council, said that the volatility of the bond market was not the direct reason for Trump to suspend tariffs, but it may have added "a little urgency" to the decision. "Everything is going on in an orderly manner. There is no doubt that the Treasury market reacted in this way yesterday. You know, it's time to act, I think, maybe more urgently. But it will happen," Hassett said. Overnight on Wednesday, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield rose to more than 4.5%, the 30-year Treasury yield soared to more than 5%, and bond prices plummeted. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Bond Market Volatility Adds Urgency to Tariff Decision
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett stated that while the volatility in the bond market was not the direct cause for U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to pause tariff imposition, it may have added a sense of urgency to the decision. Hassett remarked that everything is proceeding in an orderly manner, and the reaction of the treasury market yesterday indicated that it was time to act, possibly with increased urgency. 

On Wednesday night, the yield on the U.S. 10-year Treasury bond rose above 4.5%, while the 30-year Treasury bond yield surged past 5%, leading to a sharp decline in bond prices.

data.title: In the past 24 hours, Solana’s DEX transaction volume surpassed other chains and ranked first
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Onchain Lens data, Solana's DEX transaction volume surpassed other chains and ranked first in the past 24 hours. Specifically: Solana's DEX transaction volume was US$2.774 billion; Ethereum's DEX transaction volume was US$2.744 billion; Arbitrum's DEX transaction volume was US$1.005 billion; Base's DEX transaction volume was US$970.1 million; BSC's DEX transaction volume was US$953.3 million.

data.title: LBank has launched CRYPTOAI U-margined Perpetual Contract
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, LBank has launched the CRYPTOAI U-based perpetual contract at 18:40 (SGT) on April 10, 2025, with a maximum leverage of 20 times.
